Advice concerning filing requirements
This memorandum is to confirm my advice to you and Kevin Monroe
on August a, 1977 concerning the filing requirements of Government
code Sections 86204l1 and anus.
You stated that two legislators and their controlled committees
who filed consolidated campaign statements in connection with the
April/May 1977 Les Angela: municipal elections did not file off-year
campaign statements in July 1977 with your office.

sections.question: Whether two legislators and their controlled committees who filed consolidated campaign statements in connection with the April/May 1977 Los Angeles municipal elections are exempt from filing off-year campaign statements in July 1977 under Government Code Sections 84206 and 84204.1.
sections.conclusion: The legislators and their committees are required to file off-year statements under Section 84206. They should have filed abbreviated statements under Section 84204.1 in connection with the Los Angeles municipal elections. If a committee files a complete statement instead of an abbreviated statement, it is still required to comply with Section 84206 and file an off-year statement for that period. The staff recommends that these legislators and their committees not be assessed late filing penalties given that the filing requirements of Section 84204.1 were only recently clarified.
sections.question_synthetic: Are legislators and their controlled committees who filed consolidated campaign statements in connection with local municipal elections exempt from filing subsequent off-year campaign statements?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC advises that the legislators and their controlled committees are required to file both abbreviated statements for the municipal election and off-year statements under Section 84206, and recommends waiving late filing penalties due to recent clarification of the filing requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains two separate items: primary advice letter (main content) and secondary memorandum about Form 51A. Some OCR quality issues but core question and conclusion sections are clear. Dated August 18, 1977 with signature from Nan Ematson, Special Compliance Representative.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether two legislators and their controlled committees who filed consolidated campaign statements in connection with the April/May 1977 Los Angeles municipal elections are exempt from filing off-year campaign statements in July 1977 under Government Code Sections 84206 and 84204.1.

CONCLUSION: The legislators and their committees are required to file off-year statements under Section 84206. They should have filed abbreviated statements under Section 84204.1 in connection with the Los Angeles municipal elections. If a committee files a complete statement instead of an abbreviated statement, it is still required to comply with Section 84206 and file an off-year statement for that period. The staff recommends that these legislators and their committees not be assessed late filing penalties given that the filing requirements of Section 84204.1 were only recently clarified.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to David Pitman's query regarding filing requirements for legislators and their controlled committees under Government Code Sections 84206 and 84204.1, concluding that off-year statements are required despite prior consolidated municipal election filings. The document also includes a separate memorandum regarding Form 51A (Lobbyist Activity Authorization) revisions.
